headline: Lost Monkey Species Found in Congo Reveals Secrets Still Hidden in Nature
meta_description: Scientists in the DR Congo have identified a new species of colobus monkey with striking orange lips, only the fifth such discovery in 75 years of African rese…
summary_short: A new colobus monkey species with distinctive orange lips has been identified in the Congo Basin, offering a rare window into how much biodiversity remains undocumented.
body:
Scientists have confirmed the existence of a previously unknown colobus monkey species in the remote Lomami National Park of the Democratic Republic of Congo—a discovery that reminds us how much of nature still awaits human eyes. Named Colobus congoensis and called "Likweli" by locals, the small black-furred primate bears striking orange lips and white facial markings. The finding represents only the fifth new colobus monkey species identified in 75 years of research across Africa, making it a genuinely rare moment in modern zoology. Genetic and anatomical analyses show the animal diverged from its closest relative, the black colobus monkey, between 4 and 5 million years ago—evidence of a long evolutionary independence.

The monkey's journey to scientific recognition began modestly. Researchers captured a partial photograph of the creature in 2008, but it took a decade before a clearer image sparked serious investigation. Now, with 114 confirmed sightings recorded between 2018 and 2022 across roughly 900 square miles, scientists estimate its range is unusually small compared to related species. Congolese scientist Junior Amboko, a co-author of the research, reflected on the emotional weight of the discovery: "This highlights the extraordinary biodiversity of my homeland and how much remains undocumented." Researchers interviewed residents across 52 villages near the monkey's habitat—only 8 had ever encountered it.

While the discovery brings genuine scientific excitement, it arrives with conservation concerns. The animal's limited range and local hunting pressure have led researchers to recommend listing it as Endangered. The story underscores a deeper truth: in an age when we assume the world has been thoroughly mapped and catalogued, nature still harbors profound secrets, waiting patiently in remote forests for the right moment of discovery.
quick_facts: New colobus monkey species found in DR Congo's Lomami National Park
Only the 5th colobus species identified in 75 years of African research
114 confirmed sightings recorded between 2018 and 2022 across ~900 square miles
Distinctive features: black fur, striking orange lips, white facial markings
Diverged from closest relative 4 to 5 million years ago
